z>pos_label=2 is not a valid label. It should be one of \[0, 1\]r
Target is multilabel-indicator but average='binary'. Please choose another average setting, one of \[None, 'micro', 'macro', 'weighted', 'samples'\].r
Target is multiclass but average='binary'. Please choose another average setting, one of \[None, 'micro', 'macro', 'weighted'\].r
zJSamplewise metrics are not available outside of multilabel classification.r
Note that pos_label \(set to 3\) is ignored when average != 'binary' \(got 'micro'\). You may use labels=\[pos_label\] to specify a single positive class.r

Jaccard is ill-defined and being set to 0.0 in samples with no true or predicted labels. Use `zero_division` parameter to control this behavior.r

)	r8
4test_precision_recall_f1_no_labels_average_none_warn
d	d	g
d	d	g
d	d	g
d	d	g
d	d	g
d	d	g
Precision and F-score are ill-defined and being set to 0.0 in labels with no predicted samples. Use `zero_division` parameter to control this behavior.r
Recall and F-score are ill-defined and being set to 0.0 in labels with no true samples. Use `zero_division` parameter to control this behavior.z
Precision and F-score are ill-defined and being set to 0.0 in samples with no predicted labels. Use `zero_division` parameter to control this behavior.r3
Recall and F-score are ill-defined and being set to 0.0 in samples with no true labels. Use `zero_division` parameter to control this behavior.z
Precision and F-score are ill-defined and being set to 0.0 due to no predicted samples. Use `zero_division` parameter to control this behavior.r
Recall and F-score are ill-defined and being set to 0.0 due to no true samples. Use `zero_division` parameter to control this behavior.r
always)
popr{

d	d	g
)test_prf_no_warnings_if_zero_division_setW
zzRecall is ill-defined and being set to 0.0 due to no true samples. Use `zero_division` parameter to control this behavior.)

NTrv
Precision is ill-defined and being set to 0.0 due to no predicted samples. Use `zero_division` parameter to control this behavior.)
test_precision_warnings

F-score is ill-defined and being set to 0.0 due to no true nor predicted samples. Use `zero_division` parameter to control this behavior.)

:test_hinge_loss_multiclass_missing_labels_with_labels_none
The shape of pred_decision cannot be 1d arraywith a multiclass target. pred_decision shape must be (n_samples, n_classes), that is (7, 3). Got: (7,)r
The shape of pred_decision is not consistent with the number of classes. With a multiclass target, pred_decision shape must be (n_samples, n_classes), that is (7, 3). Got: (7, 2))
